summary: Gruber reviews Apple's September 2018 event introducing the iPhone XS, XS Max, XR, and Apple Watch Series 4. He considers the Series 4 watch the standout product, calling it a landmark redesign comparable to the iPhone 4's leap, particularly for its edge-to-edge display and ECG feature. The iPhone XR surprised him by being a true premium device rather than the mid-range phone he expected, differing from the XS mainly in its LCD screen and single camera. He devotes significant attention to the confusing X-based naming scheme, arguing Apple knows most people will mispronounce the names but simply doesn't care. He also reports that AirPower is in serious trouble due to thermal issues with its multi-coil design.
key_points: Apple Watch Series 4 is a landmark redesign paralleling the iPhone 4's leap — the new small 40mm watch has a larger display than the old large 42mm model
iPhone XR is not a mid-range phone like the 5C was; it shares the A12 chip and same wide-angle camera as the XS, making it a genuine premium device
The X-as-Roman-numeral naming scheme is fundamentally broken — putting two alphabetic characters together and expecting one to be read as a numeral is asking too much
AirPower is reportedly in deep trouble due to thermal problems, with engineers who predicted the multi-coil design couldn't work now vindicated
The ECG and fall detection features represent Apple genuinely caring about health outcomes while also wanting to sell watches — both things can be true simultaneously
money_quotes: The Series 4 watch is a landmark redesign.
A sharp-cornered rectangular display doesn't look good on a round-cornered device.
We know this feature is going to save lives.

Both of these things can be — and I believe are — true: Apple wants to make a ton of money selling Apple Watches, and Apple, institutionally, from engineers and designers right up to C-level executives, cares deeply about this health and fitness stuff.
The XR is not a new mid-range iPhone like the iPhone 5C was.
To put two alphabetic characters next to each other and expect people to treat one as a Roman numeral and the other as a letter is too much.
They think the X's look cool and they go with their guts and that's all there is to it.
AirPower really is well and truly fucked.
key_insight: The Apple Watch Series 4 represents a landmark redesign that parallels the iPhone 4's generational leap, while the iPhone XR defied expectations by being a true premium device rather than a mid-range compromise.
